title: Generic Interface / Link Down — Standard Operating Procedure
description: No high-confidence vendor-specific remedy was found. Follow this generic SOP to diagnose and restore a down interface or physical link.

steps.action: Verify the interface state on the device
steps.expected_output: Interface status line and protocol line showing 'down/down' or 'admin down'
steps.notes: Confirm whether this is an admin-down or a line-protocol-down condition.

steps.action: Check physical layer — cable, SFP, and port-level errors
steps.expected_output: Input/output error counts; CRC errors indicate layer-1 issue
steps.notes: Replace cable or SFP if CRC/input errors are high.

steps.action: Verify CDP/LLDP neighbour on the affected port
steps.expected_output: Neighbour device ID, IP address, and port
steps.notes: No neighbour output confirms the far-end device is also down or the link is broken.

steps.action: Check interface configuration for admin-down
steps.expected_output: No 'shutdown' keyword should be present if the port should be active
steps.notes: If 'shutdown' is present, bring up with 'no shutdown' after change-control approval.

steps.action: Bounce the interface after hardware checks pass
steps.expected_output: Interface transitions to 'up/up'
steps.notes: Only after confirming no hardware fault. Log the bounce in the change record.

steps.action: Confirm traffic restoration and monitor for flapping
steps.expected_output: Non-zero input/output rate indicating traffic flow
steps.notes: If interface flaps repeatedly escalate to network hardware team.

escalation: Escalate to network hardware team if physical layer issues persist after SFP/cable replacement.

title: Generic Routing / BGP Session Down — Standard Operating Procedure
description: No high-confidence vendor-specific remedy was found. Follow this generic SOP to diagnose BGP or IGP routing failures.

steps.action: Check BGP neighbour state
steps.expected_output: Neighbour state should be 'Established'; anything else requires investigation
steps.notes: Note the 'Up/Down' timer — a recent reset indicates a recent disruption.

steps.action: Verify reachability to the BGP peer IP
steps.expected_output: 100% success rate
steps.notes: If ping fails, the underlay is broken — fix the transport layer first.

steps.action: Review BGP notifications and error codes in logs
steps.expected_output: Last error code and sub-code; notification reason
steps.notes: Common causes: hold-timer expiry, AS mismatch, auth failure, policy change.

steps.action: Confirm BGP authentication key matches on both sides
steps.expected_output: Matching password or keychain configuration
steps.notes: MD5 password mismatch causes TCP sessions to fail silently.

steps.action: Clear BGP session (soft reset preferred)
steps.expected_output: Session re-establishes within hold-time
steps.notes: Use hard reset only if soft reset fails. Hard reset interrupts traffic.

escalation: Escalate to senior network engineer if BGP session does not re-establish after soft reset and underlay is confirmed healthy.

title: Generic Device Unreachable / High CPU — Standard Operating Procedure
description: No high-confidence vendor-specific remedy was found. Follow this generic SOP for device unreachability or high CPU conditions.

steps.action: Attempt out-of-band (console/OOB) access to the device
steps.expected_output: Console login prompt
steps.notes: OOB access bypasses the data plane and confirms whether the device is fully unresponsive.

steps.action: Check CPU utilisation and identify high-CPU processes
steps.expected_output: Top processes by 5-second/1-minute/5-minute CPU percentage
steps.notes: Identify the responsible process. Common culprits: BGP scanner, routing table recalculation, SNMP polling.

steps.action: Review system logs for crash/exception messages
steps.expected_output: Traceback, exception, or OOM messages
steps.notes: If crash occurred, collect core dump before rebooting.

steps.action: Check memory utilisation
steps.expected_output: Processes with highest memory allocation
steps.notes: Low free memory can cause process restarts and device instability.

steps.action: Reduce SNMP/management plane load if CPU is high
steps.expected_output: SNMP packet counts — excessive polling can drive high CPU
steps.notes: Temporarily disable non-critical SNMP polling from NMS if CPU is > 80%.

steps.action: Schedule reload if device is unresponsive and OOB access unavailable
steps.expected_output: Device reloads and becomes reachable
steps.notes: Only with change-control approval. Confirm impact scope before reloading.

escalation: Escalate to vendor TAC if device remains unreachable after OOB access attempt and reload.

title: Generic Thermal / Hardware Fault — Standard Operating Procedure
description: No high-confidence vendor-specific remedy was found. Follow this generic SOP for temperature alarms or hardware component faults.

steps.action: Check environmental status — temperature and fan speed
steps.expected_output: Temperature readings per module with status (Normal / Warning / Critical)
steps.notes: Confirm which components are over threshold.

steps.action: Verify data-centre cooling is operational
steps.expected_output: DCIM/BMS confirms CRAC units are active and airflow is unobstructed
steps.notes: Check physical rack for airflow blockage — cable management, blanking panels.

steps.action: Check fan trays and power supplies
steps.expected_output: All fans at expected RPM; no failed/absent fan trays
steps.notes: A failed fan tray can cause localised overheating even when room cooling is fine.

steps.action: Reduce load if thermal condition is critical
steps.expected_output: Traffic redistributed to other devices in the cluster
steps.notes: Shift traffic away from the affected device using routing policy before hardware replacement.

escalation: Escalate to data-centre facilities team and vendor hardware support immediately for critical thermal alarms.

title: Generic Security / Access Control Fault — Standard Operating Procedure
description: No high-confidence vendor-specific remedy was found. Follow this generic SOP for authentication failures, ACL misconfigurations, or security policy violations.

steps.action: Review authentication failure logs
steps.expected_output: Source IP, username, and failure reason for each failed attempt
steps.notes: Distinguish between misconfig (legitimate user locked out) and attack (unknown source IPs).

steps.action: Verify AAA server reachability
steps.expected_output: Authentication passed / server reachable
steps.notes: If RADIUS/TACACS is unreachable, local fallback authentication may be needed.

steps.action: Check ACL for unintended deny entries
steps.expected_output: Hit counters on deny entries indicate mismatched traffic
steps.notes: High hit counts on deny rules confirm misapplied or overly broad ACL.

steps.action: Isolate source if brute-force attack is suspected
steps.expected_output: Source IP blocked at perimeter firewall or ingress ACL
steps.notes: Engage security operations team for incident response if attack is confirmed.

escalation: Escalate to security operations team immediately if attack pattern is confirmed.

title: Generic Network Fault — Standard Operating Procedure
description: No high-confidence vendor-specific remedy was found and the fault category could not be determined. Follow this baseline SOP for any unclassified network event.

steps.action: Confirm the alert is genuine — check monitoring tool for false-positive indicators
steps.expected_output: Alert is corroborated by at least one other data source (SNMP trap, syslog, NMS)
steps.notes: Do not action alerts that are not independently corroborated.

steps.action: Identify the affected device and collect basic status
steps.expected_output: Device type, IOS/OS version, uptime, and last reload reason
steps.notes: Uptime < 10 minutes indicates a recent reload — check for crash.

steps.action: Review recent syslog messages on the device
steps.expected_output: Recent error or warning messages with timestamps
steps.notes: Correlate timestamps with the alert window.

steps.action: Check interface and protocol status
steps.expected_output: All expected interfaces are up/up
steps.notes: Any down interface should be investigated first as it may be the root cause.

steps.action: Verify management plane connectivity
steps.expected_output: 100% ping success from management IP
steps.notes: If management reachability is intermittent, check management VRF routing.

steps.action: Escalate with collected data if root cause is not identified
steps.expected_output: Ticket created with: device name, alarm details, syslog excerpt, and steps already taken
steps.notes: Attach show-tech output to the escalation ticket.

escalation: Escalate to senior NOC engineer with collected show-tech and syslog data if fault is not resolved within SLA window.

title: Virtual Machine Stunned & MongoDB Timeout Recovery SOP
description: Recommended SOP to recover from hypervisor storage latency and stunned Virtual Machine state.

steps.action: Verify storage latency and status on host ESX-07
steps.expected_output: Datastore latency details and storage path status
steps.notes: Verify if storage connectivity is stable or if paths are failing.

steps.action: Check VM power and stun logs in host vmkernel.log
steps.expected_output: VM stunned messages and duration
steps.notes: Check how long the VM was stunned. High durations explain service timeouts.

steps.action: Check MongoDB service state on the guest VM
steps.expected_output: mongod.service status (active, inactive, or failed)
steps.notes: If service failed due to timeout, it needs to be restarted.

steps.action: Restart MongoDB service if inactive or failed
steps.expected_output: mongod.service running
steps.notes: Ensure service restarts and verify TCP port 27017 status.

steps.action: Verify MongoDB replica set synchronization status
steps.expected_output: Replica set health status showing members as PRIMARY or SECONDARY
steps.notes: Confirm heartbeats are restored and replica lag is decreasing.

escalation: Escalate to storage engineering and DBA teams if datastore latency remains above 30ms or replica sync fails.

title: Fibre Cable Cut — Outside Plant Recovery SOP
description: Standard operating procedure for handling a confirmed physical fibre cut in the outside plant or long-haul network.

steps.action: Run OTDR (Optical Time-Domain Reflectometer) diagnostics from the nearest optical distribution frame.
steps.expected_output: Distance to fault in meters.
steps.notes: Compare the fault distance against the outside plant GIS mapping to isolate the break location.

steps.action: Verify if multiple circuits on the same physical conduit are down simultaneously.
steps.expected_output: Multiple links down sharing the same route.
steps.notes: Correlates the fault to a shared conduit breach.

steps.action: Dispatch Outside Plant (OSP) civil works / splicing crew to the fault location.
steps.expected_output: Crew dispatched.
steps.notes: Provide the crew with OTDR distance and GPS coordinates from GIS.

steps.action: Verify light levels post-splice.
steps.expected_output: Rx power within acceptable dBm limits.
steps.notes: Do not clear the ticket until Rx power is stable for 30 minutes.

escalation: Escalate to the Regional Fiber Manager and Carrier Relations if third-party leased fibre.

title: Copper Cable Replacement SOP
description: Procedure for handling physical breaks, open circuits, or impedance faults in copper (Cat5/6/RJ45) cabling.

steps.action: Run TDR (Time Domain Reflectometer) test on the interface.
steps.cli:
test cable-diagnostics tdr interface {iface}
show cable-diagnostics tdr interface {iface}
steps.expected_output: Open or Short circuit at X meters.
steps.notes: Confirms physical cable damage rather than auto-negotiation failure.

steps.action: Dispatch local site-support technician to the rack/floor location.
steps.notes: Provide exact port mapping and fault distance.

steps.action: Replace the damaged copper patch cable or re-terminate the horizontal run.
steps.expected_output: Cable replaced.
steps.notes: Ensure Cat6/6A is used for replacement to maintain 10G+ speeds if applicable.

steps.action: Verify interface link status and speed negotiation.
steps.expected_output: connected, a-full, a-1000
steps.notes: Clear interface counters after replacement.

escalation: Escalate to Site Facilities if the horizontal cable run in the wall/ceiling requires replacement.

title: Control Plane DDoS / Attack Mitigation SOP
description: Procedure for mitigating an active Denial of Service attack overwhelming the network device control plane CPU.

steps.action: Identify the top talker or attack vector hitting the CPU.
steps.expected_output: IP input, ARP input, or SSH processes spiking CPU.
steps.notes: Determine if the attack is ARP flood, SSH brute force, or generic IP flood.

steps.action: Check Control Plane Policing (CoPP) drop counters.
steps.expected_output: High drop rate on default or management classes.
steps.notes: If CoPP is not dropping the traffic, the policy needs adjustment.

steps.action: Apply an ACL to drop the malicious source IP or rate-limit the specific protocol.
steps.cli:
configure terminal
 ip access-list extended BLOCK-ATTACK
 deny ip host {attacker_ip} any
 permit ip any any
 interface {ingress_iface}
 ip access-group BLOCK-ATTACK in
steps.expected_output: Traffic dropped, CPU drops back to baseline.
steps.notes: Use caution not to lock out management access.

steps.action: If attack is distributed, activate BGP RTBH (Remotely Triggered Black Hole).
steps.expected_output: Traffic to target dropped at network edge.
steps.notes: Only use RTBH if the target IP can afford to be taken offline.

escalation: Immediately engage the Security Operations Center (SOC) and Tier 3 Network Security.
